One thing that actually kind of stood out to what you were sharing so far, you know, like positioning that like magazine approach really resonates with me and how we think here.
Our mindset is like, how do we help our customers operate more like publishers?
We've seen a lot of benefits to building teams in that way when we were all in-house, where a lot of marketers who were in-house had built, you know, this team here.

But I've just seen a lot of challenges along the way of doing that, like building that right team, coordinating on the right topics, figuring out that right editorial layer to produce quality consistently, then creating the user experience.
the point being, there's just like a lot of friction to scaling that out.
And, you know, at times what we find is some brands may only look at it as like just high volume blog content and very SEO centric.
And to us, that's not the mindset we want to apply, you know, for us.
We want to really understand your personas at a deep level, their jobs to be done, the questions associated with their opportunities and pain points associated to those jobs to be done.
If we can provide really amazing, engaging answers to that, that will pay off in organic traffic, but also just build a healthy, organic engine where that content has value that extends beyond that.
And so for us, as we thought about how do we bring AI workflows into what we do as growth practitioners, there are so many different ideas.

You know, what you were mentioning on HubSpot and like, you know, different ways we can play around with where automation sits with email, whatever it may be, you know, no shortage of ideas or things we've experimented with.
But what we have found is what works best for our engagements, again, thinking of that mindset of how can we help our customers operate like publishers is starting with that editorial layer.
And for us, what that means is longer form content.
And the purpose behind that is that we just find that there's a lot of benefits from starting there.

In terms of, again, understanding your personas, like really starting to dig into those topics, calibrating on what amazing content looks like, what's that right tone, voice, structure, et cetera, getting those signals back and continuing to evolve what topics are engaging the right audience.
There's just some rich learnings that can come from that, that can inform everything else rather than jump into those other things that can help us identify programmatic bets.
But it doesn't mean that we have to go through that layer for all customers to get to a programmatic bet.
What that journey looks like can be different for each customer, honestly.
But we just find a lot of times if we start there, we have a lot of foundational things to build upon.
You can start to think about distribution.
We can start to bring in email, lead magnets, social, et cetera.
It's just oftentimes we won't, it's very rare where we would start engagement where that is the core thing we're doing as an offering.
Sometimes we'll entertain it, but honestly, it's just, that's just.
It's not the style of engagement we're focused on today.
And that's informed the team we've built here.

Because what we have found testing different things, you know, hiring at different levels, training, et cetera, you know, the, well, you can build, you know, the most amazing set of workflows.
So if your inputs are crap, your outputs are going to be crap as well.

And so, you know, really building that right strategy and thinking iteratively over time of how we're leveraging these capabilities is how we've been able to have a lot of success with our client base.

Okay.
Yeah, we can send you the SoW that just like breaks it down so you can, you know, look at it a bit more clearly.
Usually what we do from here is, you know, schedule a follow-up to dig in a little bit deeper for that.
I'm happy to, you know, you mentioned a few competitors.
We can chat through some opportunities we see and just take it from there.
Oh, and as well as we typically spin up a Slack channel just for like comms if you're open to it.
It just helps rather than going back and forth in email.
Yeah, makes sense.
Okay.
This is comparable to our existing agency.
They're at about like an 8K per month retainer.
We've just done a ton of strategy work and I'm like, just, I'm keen to just move to shipping quickly.
Which was the question behind my question on the sprint.
Like I'm really looking more for a programmatic SEO partner that we can spin up quickly.
So that's why I'm more interested in getting an option that's more execution oriented.
Yeah, I mean, I can definitely take it back with folks like Marcel and see where it can be flexible.
You know, people are coming to us at varying phases.
We typically don't go under six weeks, but if it's something like four and, you know, we have conviction that, hey, we do have a lot of details we need and this is legit.
You know, all the intention here is to set the right stage for a long-term partnership, right?
Yeah.
What enables us to do that, we're game.
I mean, under the hood, think there's just a lot that you'd be able to potentially use.
Like we're kind of looking at like click play on some strategy that's already been very well mapped in my opinion.
Sure.
Sure.
So I'm pulling up this, Figma, just to give you a state of play of we've mapped out heaps of content that is not yet being produced, some of which has been shipped.
Clusters, we've got, I'd say, we're swimming in strategy.
Strategy is not where you're feeling the pain at the moment.
That's correct.
Yeah, but what attracted me about you folks was like, how can we just like, increase the velocity and do it in a way that is next gen?
Because I feel like we're sitting in 2010s era strategy, or maybe not strategies, 2010s era execution, let's say strategies relatively consistent over time, you may disagree with that.
From an execution perspective, we're kind of, I think, behind the times.
Makes sense.
Cool.
Interesting, Kyle, because it's not every day, right, that we get customers like that.
Rob, the reason why we kind of pulled back, like, beginning of the year, we grew really.
And the strategy sprint was essentially embedded in the first two to four weeks of the engagement.
Yeah.
Because everybody wanted to rush through the first week and start publishing by week two.
Kyle cracked me if I'm wrong, maybe week three and four.
And so there were expectations there that were unrealistic or hard to meet, right?
And that kind of didn't help us.
Give me different flavors.
Yeah, a variety of different reasons.
I do have to hop.
I've been keeping a few folks waiting.
But let's see if we can get like time.
I don't know if Wednesday works for Rob, but maybe that's a good time.
can wrap it up, Kyle.
Yeah.
All right.
There's also one other client I'm working with that I'm going throw in the mix called Argo.
So since you're talking to the float, that's great.
Yeah.
There's another one called Argo that is nowhere near where SingleKey is.
Yeah.
But it's a venture-backed kind of gold investing platform in the States that I also would like to get a quote for.
I think SingleKey is probably more straight down the middle because we're already heavily investing in half.
Traction, but let me throw that one out there, too.
Cool.
Hey, let's chat.
Great meeting you, Rob.
Happy Friday.
Looking forward to chatting.
Bye.
Thanks, Kyle.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introductory call between SingleKey and GrowthX to explore potential partnership for scaling SEO efforts in the US market.](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [SingleKey has strong SEO performance in Canada (\~$300K MRR), seeking to replicate success in more competitive US market](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=150.0)
  - [GrowthX offers AI-powered content creation workflows combined with high-level editorial expertise](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=622.0)
  - [SingleKey has extensive strategy work completed, primarily seeking execution support to increase content velocity](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=597.0)
  - [GrowthX typically starts with a strategy sprint, but may be flexible given SingleKey's existing strategy](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=1531.0)

## Topics

### SingleKey's Current SEO Performance and Goals

  - [Dominant in Canadian market: \~$300K MRR from SEO, top rankings for key terms](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
  - [US expansion goal: Achieve parity with Canadian SEO performance within 1 year](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=263.0)
  - [Target: Grow from $10M annual revenue (95% Canada) to $50-100M potential in US](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=507.0)

### GrowthX's Approach and Capabilities

  - [AI-powered workflows combined with high-level editorial expertise](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=1157.0)
  - [Team includes former TechCrunch editor-in-chief and experienced SEO professionals](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=1163.0)
  - [Modular workflows for research, content creation, and programmatic SEO](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=1334.0)
  - [Typically starts with 6-8 week strategy sprint ($20K) before ongoing execution ($9-15K/month)](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=1839.0)

### Content Creation Process

  - [Focus on longer-form, high-quality content as foundation](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=1070.0)
  - [Use of AI for research, brief creation, and initial drafts](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=1419.0)
  - [Human editorial layer for quality control and strategic alignment](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=1464.0)
  - [Capability to scale to 5-15 articles per week, with potential for higher volume in programmatic efforts](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=1864.0)

### Programmatic SEO Example

  - [Showcased RAMP per-diem pages project](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=1730.0)
  - [Built calculator function and navigation for state/city-level pages](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=1734.0)
  - [Combination of templatized and generated content with scraped data](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=1754.0)
  - [Resulted in significant traffic growth within \~1 month](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=1772.0)

### SingleKey's Current Situation

  - [Extensive strategy work already completed](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=2100.0)
  - [Seeking execution partner to increase content velocity](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=2046.0)
  - [Current agency at \~$8K/month retainer](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=2031.0)
  - [Interest in "next-gen" execution methods to modernize approach](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=2135.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX to explore flexibility on engagement structure given SingleKey's existing strategy work](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=2059.0)
  - [Schedule follow-up call to dig deeper into opportunities and potential partnership](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=2188.0)
  - [GrowthX to send Statement of Work (SoW) for SingleKey's review](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=2001.0)
  - [Discuss potential for shorter strategy sprint (e.g., 4 weeks) to expedite execution phase](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=2040.0)
  - [Consider additional quote for Rob's other client, Argo (venture-backed gold investing platform)](https://fathom.video/share/gqZj1j6YnzpaFXkmSyEAmxBs9U_4GK4k?tab=summary&timestamp=2196.0)

action_items.description: Send SoW to Rob detailing GrowthX services/pricing
Schedule follow-up meeting w/ Rob to discuss opportunities/next steps
Set up Slack channel for comms w/ Rob/SingleKey
Discuss w/ Marcel re: flexible execution-only option for SingleKey, bypassing strategy sprint
Prepare quote for Argo (gold investing platform) SEO services
